forbiddenClaims: objective truth or relevance-authority ranking of publishers or results
AI summary accuracy percentage, confidence score, coverage metric, or index-size figure
AI summary presented as the publisher's verdict, Newswire's editorial pick, or a source of record
trending tickers, related topics, or country hubs presented as forecasts, investment signals, or buy/sell recommendations
fabricated result totals or synthetic results padding a zero-results or slow-query state
sign-up or follow-list required to run an exploratory query
affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, rating agency, or data vendor

rubricAnchors.score1: Search claims an objective ranking or presents discovery as a forecast, or AI-summary results masquerade as publisher articles.
rubricAnchors.score3: Ranking and discovery are mostly hedged but one result reads as an authority pick or AI summaries blur into publisher results.
rubricAnchors.score5: Ranking is inferred relevance, AI-summary results are visually distinct and source-attributed, discovery is labelled machine-assembled, and exploratory search works without sign-up.

rubricAnchors.score1: search-input, results-list, and discovery-hub share no common direction system and disagree on how ranking or AI summaries are labelled.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the three screens but the publisher label, timestamp, or AI-summary marker is applied inconsistently between list and discovery.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, with a consistent inferred-relevance framing, consistent AI-summary-vs-publisher distinction, and consistent attribution on every result and hub item.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the search-input, results-list, or discovery-hub states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved zero-results, typo-corrected, query-slow, or country-hub-selected states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including zero-results, typo-corrected, query-slow, results-loading-error, trending-tickers-expanded, related-topics-expanded, and country-hub-selected states with their mobile rules.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing finance-search surface that ranks sources by authority or presents discovery as investment signals.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common search patterns but the inferred-relevance and machine-assembled-discovery treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes inferred ranking, machine-assembled discovery, and AI-summary-vs-publisher separation legible without claiming authority or coverage.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no zero-results, query-slow, or results-loading-error states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of zero-results or query-slow.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with zero-results, typo-corrected, query-slow, and results-loading-error states that keep filters and recent queries reachable and never fabricate totals.

rubricAnchors.score1: An objective-ranking, accuracy, coverage, index-size, or investment-signal claim appears unsupported, or a zero-results state is padded with synthetic results.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one result reads as an authority pick or one discovery item reads as a forecast.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, ranking is inferred relevance, discovery is machine-assembled, AI summaries are distinct and source-attributed, and no authority, accuracy, coverage, or index-size claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: Search ranking is an inferred relevance signal, never objective truth; any authority-ranking or 'most correct result' framing is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline.
AI-summary results must be visually distinct from publisher-article results; an output that blurs them fails product-appropriateness and cross-screen-coherence.
Discovery must read as machine-assembled and inferred; an output that presents trending tickers or country hubs as forecasts or investment signals fails evidence-discipline.

Honest recovery is load-bearing: an output that fabricates result totals, pads zero-results with synthetic items, or claims a slow query returned results fails evidence-discipline and accessibility-and-failure-states.
Exploratory search must not gate on sign-up or a follow list; an output that blocks a new user's first query fails product-appropriateness.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes the inferred-ranking and machine-assembled-discovery constraints, not the visual identity.
